title: verbascum phrygium
intro: Verbascum phrygium, also known as Phrygian mullein, is a biennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its tall stature and large, yellow flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and poultices, which have been valued for their soothing and healing properties, providing relief from pain, inflammation, and skin conditions. Additionally, Verbascum phrygium has been used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being, with its herbal preparations often used to treat colds, fever, and other infections.
